interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, navigating HR domain complexity
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design a 360-review system with manager, peer, and direct-report inputs
Design goal-tracking with cascading from company → team → individual
Design engagement-survey aggregation that preserves anonymity

coding: At Lattice, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Lattice scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, navigating hr domain complexity.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a 360-review system with manager, peer, and direct-report inputs.
Design goal-tracking with cascading from company → team → individual.
Design engagement-survey aggregation that preserves anonymity.
values: Lattice succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Lattice compare to Culture Amp or 15Five?

Lattice Interview Guide (2026): HR Tech and Performance Management

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Lattice
lattice.com ↗

Lattice is the leading performance management and people analytics platform — used by 5000+ companies for reviews, goals, 1:1s, and engagement surveys. The interview is enterprise-SaaS-flavored with focus on workflow design, complex data modeling, and the realities of building tools used by HR teams.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a 360-review system with manager, peer, and direct-report inputs

- Design goal-tracking with cascading from company → team → individual

- Design engagement-survey aggregation that preserves anonymity

- Coding: medium DSA, often with workflow or graph flavor

- Behavioral: customer focus, navigating HR domain complexity

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $150K–$185K total

- Senior SE: $215K–$285K

- Staff: $310K–$410K

- Principal: $430K–$570K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in TypeScript/React for frontend; Ruby on Rails for backend

- Understand survey design and anonymity-preserving aggregation

- Brush up on workflow engines and approval-graph patterns

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Lattice remote-friendly?

Hybrid in San Francisco, NYC, London, and others. Many engineering roles fully remote within supported countries.

How does Lattice compare to Culture Amp or 15Five?

Lattice has the broadest product (reviews, goals, 1:1s, engagement). Culture Amp leads on engagement; 15Five on continuous performance. Comp is comparable.

What is the engineering culture?

Mid-stage SaaS — collaborative, customer-driven, healthy work-life balance. Strong product-engineering partnership.
